sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Inter are in excellent shape, having won their last three matches and remaining unbeaten in their last five, scoring consistently with an average of 1.8 goals per game. Napoli, too, are on a strong run, unbeaten in their last five matches, with two wins and a draw in their recent outings, netting an average of 2 goals per game. Both teams show attacking intent, but Inter's dominance in possession and shot creation gives them a slight edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Napoli have had the upper hand, securing a 3-1 victory at home and a 1-1 draw in the previous meeting. Historically, matches between these two often see both teams scoring, with 4 out of 5 recent games having goals on both sides, suggesting a competitive clash. Inter will look to reverse this trend at home, where they’ve been formidable.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: Inter’s attacking duo of Lautaro Martínez (3 goals in last 5 matches) and Hakan Çalhanoğlu will be pivotal, backed by their league-leading stats in big chances created (78 for the season). Napoli, missing key players like Frank Anguissa, will rely on Rasmus Højlund and Scott McTominay to exploit counter-attacks, but their defensive solidity (unbeaten run) will be tested against Inter’s high-pressing game (top in final third entries).

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Inter lead Serie A with 40 goals scored and rank first in corners per game (6.94), showcasing their attacking prowess and set-piece threat. Napoli, fourth with 38 points, have a balanced attack (28 goals) and a strong first-half scoring record (often first to score in recent games). Inter’s superior possession (59.4%) and passing accuracy (87%) could dictate the tempo at San Siro.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Inter’s home advantage, current form, and Sofascore’s 66% probability of a home win, they are favored to edge out Napoli in a close contest. Napoli’s resilience and recent head-to-head success suggest they won’t go down easily, but Inter’s attacking depth should secure the win. Expect a tight game with potential for goals on both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at San Siro between Inter and Napoli ended with a 1-1 scoreline, reflecting a tightly contested battle. Both teams managed to find the back of the net, with Federico Dimarco striking early for Inter and Scott McTominay equalizing for Napoli. The game has been intense, with each side showing moments of quality in attack and resilience in defense. Inter have slightly edged possession and created more entries into the final third, while Napoli have been clinical with their limited chances. As we head into the second half, both teams have everything to play for in this crucial encounter.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Inter's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Inter started the game brightly, taking the lead through Dimarco's well-taken goal. They've shown dominance in ball possession and have been more effective in penetrating the final third and penalty area. Their aerial and ground duels have been particularly strong, allowing them to maintain control for significant periods. However, they missed a big chance to extend their lead, which could haunt them if Napoli capitalize on counter-attacks. Players like Nicolò Barella have been instrumental in creating opportunities, while the defensive line has held firm against Napoli's sporadic threats.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Napoli's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Napoli responded well to going behind, with McTominay's goal showcasing their ability to strike efficiently despite limited possession. Their attacking forays have been less frequent compared to Inter, with fewer final third entries and penalty area penetrations. Defensively, they've struggled in duels, particularly in the air, which has allowed Inter to dominate physically. However, their ability to convert a big chance into a goal keeps them in the game. Eljif Elmas played a key role in setting up the equalizer, and Napoli will need more from their forwards to turn the tide.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Inter largely dictating the attacking momentum, especially in the opening minutes and towards the end of the half with sustained pressure. They had multiple spells of dominance where they pushed forward relentlessly. Napoli, on the other hand, showed brief but effective bursts of attacking intent, particularly around the time of their goal, capitalizing on quick transitions. While Inter have had more consistent attacking phases, Napoli's sharpness during key moments kept the score level.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Heading into the second half, Inter are likely to continue leveraging their possession and physical dominance to create more scoring opportunities. They will aim to exploit Napoli's weakness in duels and push for a decisive goal. Napoli, meanwhile, might focus on soaking up pressure and hitting on the counter, as they've shown they can be lethal with limited chances. Tactical adjustments could play a big role, with Inter potentially looking to tighten their finishing and Napoli seeking to shore up their defense. The game remains finely poised, but Inter's control in the first half gives them a slight edge.
